title: Agentic Engineering
description: Build, orchestrate, and secure autonomous AI agents for real-world software delivery using Google Antigravity and the Tridorian standard.

courses.title:
**Secure Agentic Development: Installation & Setup
**
courses.description: **Course Abstract:** Welcome to the foundational session of Google Antigravity (AGV). Before we can orchestrate AI agents to write code or execute terminal commands, we must establish a secure, working environment.

courses.title: AGY-102: The UI Landscape & Selection (L100)
courses.description: This course provides a comprehensive comparative tour of Google Antigravity's three main command interfaces: the standalone visual board (antigravity), the lightweight command line interface (agy), and the agent-integrated workspace editor (antigravity-ide). Learners will explore the design philosophy, target use cases, configuration setups, and selection workflows for each UI surface, culminating in a clear understanding of how to seamlessly coordinate across all three for peak agentic productivity.

courses.title: AGY-103: Workspace Design & File Security (L100)
courses.description: Learn to design secure workspace boundaries and configure file-system level access controls for autonomous AI agents. This course provides comprehensive training on directory locking, POSIX folder permissions, and designing custom SDK policies to shield sensitive user folders (such as SSH keys, desktop, documents) from agent interactions.

courses.title: AGY-104: Agent-Human Collaboration Loops (L100)
courses.description: Constructing clear instructions, managing terminal execution approvals, and learning how to safely pause, redirect, and resume an agent task.

courses.title: AGY-201: Standalone Operations (L200)
courses.description: An in-depth, hands-on guide to mastering Standalone Operations using the antigravity visual desktop board. Designed specifically for visual learners, solution architects, and technical business analysts, this course covers the configuration and management of asynchronous agent workflows, advanced prompt engineering techniques for structured document generation, and deep-dive troubleshooting of web-scraping agents via visual playback tapes. By the end of this course, you will be able to orchestrate complex multi-agent scraping pipelines, debug live selector issues, and compile clean markdown analytical dashboards with zero placeholders.

courses.title: AGY-202: AGY CLI Operations (L200)
courses.description: This course provides a comprehensive hands-on guide to mastering Google Antigravity's command line interface, agy. Built for terminal-first developers, DevOps engineers, and technical systems analysts, the course teaches you the global flag hierarchy, how to pipe local directory trees and text streams into agent contexts, the best practices for safe bulk file modifications and code refactoring using dry-run flags, and the automation of rapid batch query jobs. You will learn to integrate the agent directly into standard UNIX shell pipelines, culminating in a hands-on Specialist Capstone Challenge to earn your AGY Certified Terminal Specialist credential.

courses.title: AGY-203: Interactive Workspaces (L200)
courses.description: Welcome to AGY-203: Interactive Workspaces! This elective course is designed for Writers, Developers, and Editor Enthusiasts who want to master synchronous side-by-side co-authoring, workspace markdown navigation, and live text rewriting or code refactoring inside antigravity-ide. Learners will acquire hands-on experience using the agentic sidecar, managing workspace security boundaries, and collaborating with autonomous AI agents to construct high-quality technical documentation and codebases.

courses.title: AGY-311: Scripting with the SDK (L300)
courses.description: Learn to programmatically automate agentic workflows using the Google Antigravity SDK. This course covers initiating stateful conversation loops, managing token streams (text and thinking tokens), injecting structured context arrays, enforcing response schemas for machine-to-machine integrations, and controlling execution using lifecycle hooks and custom tools in both Python and Node.js environments.

courses.title: AGY-411: Custom MCP Servers (L400)
courses.description: A high-level systems integration and architecture course covering the design, hosting, implementation, and deployment of custom Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers. Students will master how to securely build and maintain MCP interfaces that allow autonomous AI agents to query database layers, REST microservices, and ticketing platforms under strict enterprise network boundaries, access constraints, and credential management standards.

courses.title: AGY-321: Jules VM Orchestration (L300)
courses.description: Welcome to AGY-321: Jules VM Orchestration. This course is specifically designed for Solutions Architects who are responsible for designing, deploying, and managing secure, high-performance execution environments for autonomous AI agents. In this course, you will learn to orchestrate remote pools of isolated, ephemeral virtual machine instances (Jules VMs) to execute complex, untrusted build pipelines and testing jobs. You will master VM provisioning configurations, pool resource policies, security firewalls, programmatic SDK integrations, and governance auditing.

courses.title: AGY-421: Enterprise Governance & Rules (L400)
courses.description: Welcome to AGY-421: Enterprise Governance & Rules. This L400 course is designed exclusively for Lead Architects and Delivery Directors who are responsible for establishing, monitoring, and enforcing development standards, compliance frameworks, and security boundaries across autonomous agentic teams. Throughout this course, you will learn to codify enterprise compliance, configure local workspace firewalls using .gemini.md, restrict execution commands, design multi-agent workflows, verify architectural patterns, and integrate governance audits directly into CI/CD pipelines to prevent rule drift and maintain immutable security boundaries.

courses.title: AGY-331: Advanced Container Sandboxing (L300)
courses.description: Configuring local distrobox or devcontainers environments using the portable agy-box OCI image to run high-autonomy agent loops safely.

courses.title: AGY-431: Scale & Immutable DevOps (L400)
courses.description: An advanced, hands-on masterclass for DevOps Leads and Platform Engineers. Master the architecture, deployment, and orchestration of containerized Google Antigravity environments (agy-box) across remote cloud VMs. Learn how to configure private container registries behind restrictive corporate proxies, manage TLS/SSL certificate injection, and implement secure headless CI/CD execution loops with strict policy enforcement.
